summary: Applied ADR 0284 from the latest `origin/main` baseline by converging the private Piper runtime on docker-runtime-lv3, repairing stale Docker build and compose-network failure modes discovered during the first replay, correcting Piper's canonical private port from 8099 to 8100 after proving a real collision with the already-live Temporal UI listener, and verifying the healthy `/healthz`, `/api/voices`, and `/api/tts` contracts end to end from the current 0.130.74 platform baseline.

verification.check: Focused Piper regression coverage and generation refresh
verification.observed: `make generate-platform-vars`, `uv run --with pyyaml python scripts/generate_slo_rules.py --write`, and `make generate-ops-portal` all completed after the 8100 port correction, and `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest -q tests/test_piper_runtime_role.py tests/test_piper_playbook.py tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py` then returned `45 passed in 16.21s`.

verification.check: Syntax, health-probe, and repository data-model validation
verification.observed: `make syntax-check-piper`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh health-probes`, and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` all passed on the corrected workstream branch.

verification.check: First governed replay exposed stale Docker build-network drift
verification.observed: `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=piper env=production` first failed while Docker tried to create a build endpoint on a missing bridge network; the full transcript is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0284-converge-piper-r1.txt` and directly motivated the recovery hardening now carried in `lv3.platform.piper_runtime`.

verification.check: Second governed replay exposed the real latest-main port collision
verification.observed: The second replay reached service startup and then failed because `temporal-ui` already owned `127.0.0.1:8099`; that failed converge is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0284-converge-piper-r2.txt`, and the direct `ss` plus `docker ps` confirmation is summarized in this receipt's notes.

verification.check: Corrected governed Piper replay on port 8100
verification.observed: After correcting Piper to `8100`, `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=piper env=production` succeeded with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=124 changed=8 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=25 rescued=1 ignored=0`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0284-converge-piper-r3.txt`.

`receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0284-piper-local-http-r1.txt` confirms `docker port piper 5000/tcp` published both `10.10.10.20:8100` and `127.0.0.1:8100`, `GET /healthz` returned the default voice `en_US-ryan-medium`, and `GET /api/voices` returned that voice in the service inventory.

The host-path verification in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0284-piper-host-path-tts-r1.txt` called `http://10.10.10.20:8100/api/tts?...` from `proxmox-host` and returned the valid WAV summary `{"content_type":"audio/wav","riff":"RIFF","wave":"WAVE","bytes":104492}`.

verification.check: Governed wrapper canonical-truth stop on the workstream branch
verification.observed: `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0284-live-apply-wrapper-r1.txt` shows the expected branch-local stop at `Canonical truth is stale. Run uvx --from pyyaml python scripts/canonical_truth.py --write.` because `README.md` remains intentionally protected until the exact-main integration step.

notes: This receipt is the branch-local live-apply audit trail for ADR 0284 on top of the current 0.177.112 / 0.130.74 baseline; the exact-main integration step still needs to refresh protected canonical-truth surfaces and rerun the governed wrapper from committed mainline source.

The port conflict was real latest-main drift, not an operator mistake: `temporal-ui` already published `127.0.0.1:8099->8080/tcp`, so Piper's canonical private listener had to move to `8100` for the repo to remain self-consistent.

The recovery hardening added to `lv3.platform.piper_runtime` mirrors the safer repo pattern already used elsewhere: temp build dir, `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0`, `docker build --network host`, stale Docker build-network repair, and compose retry after Docker NAT or compose-network recovery.

Merge-to-main still needs to update `README.md`, `VERSION`, `changelog.md`, `RELEASE.md`, `docs/release-notes/`, `build/platform-manifest.json`, and `versions/stack.yaml`, then rerun the governed wrapper from the exact-main tree so the canonical mainline receipt records committed-source proof.
